block_19: Most Profitable Trade of the Week
block_45: Turns out that financial bubbles are nothing new to the world of investing. You probably remember the .com rise and fall, or how about the 1929 Wall Street crash? Anyway, back in the 17th century, the South Sea Company was in the center of a giant bubble, which caused the company's share prices to collapse by the year 1720. And even before that, back in 1637, there was tulip fever. Some skilled laborer sold their tulip bulbs at a price ten times higher than their annual incomes.
